title: REAL-TIME ANOMALY DETECTION AND PREDICTIVE MAINTENANCE IN HIGH-THROUGHPUT MOLECULAR DIAGNOSTIC CARTRIDGE MANUFACTURING USING MULTI-MODAL SENSOR FUSION AND ENSEMBLE MACHINE LEARNING
author: Adetayo Folasole
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The   increasing global demand   for rapid, high-throughput molecular diagnostic testing has placed unprecedented performance and reliability expectations on cartridge manufacturing systems. These production environments operate under stringent precision, sterility, and quality requirements, where even minor deviations in thermal control, reagent dispensing, microfluidic sealing, or mechanical assembly can compromise diagnostic accuracy. From a broader perspective, real-time anomaly detection and predictive maintenance have become essential pillars of modern diagnostics manufacturing, ensuring uninterrupted output while reducing scrap rates, equipment failures, and compliance risks. This study situates advanced monitoring strategies within the wider context of Industry 4.0, where multi-modal sensing, automated analytics, and continuous validation workflows are transforming operational reliability. Narrowing the focus, the paper presents a specialized framework tailored for high-throughput molecular diagnostic cartridge production, integrating multi-modal sensor fusion with ensemble machine learning techniques. The proposed approach collects and harmonizes diverse data streams including vibration signatures, thermal profiles, laser micrometry, optical imaging, pressure curves, and reagent-flow metrics to capture the full operational envelope of assembly and verification processes. These heterogeneous data sources are processed through an ensemble of machine learning models combining random forests, gradientboosted methods, one-class SVMs, and temporal sequence models for dynamic anomaly detection. A predictive maintenance layer leverages fused sensor features and historical fault patterns to forecast component degradation and proactively schedule interventions before deviations escalate into quality failures or line downtime. The resulting system enhances real-time defect detection, reduces false alarms, and provides interpretable alerts for quality engineers and process technicians, thereby accelerating root-cause isolation and improving regulatory compliance. By merging multi-modal sensing with robust ensemble analytics, this study demonstrates a scalable pathway toward fault-tolerant, data-driven manufacturing in molecular diagnostics.
